name: fastapi_python_ceiling_updated_plus_retained
answer: At that point, Python 3.13 was the newest explicitly supported Python line in the release notes. FastAPI 0.118.0 added support for Python 3.13.

name: fastapi_python_support_updated_plus_retained
answer: FastAPI 0.125.0 dropped support for Python 3.8, so Python 3.9+ remained supported. The same release-note stream was already moving FastAPI docs and internals toward Python 3.10 syntax.

name: pytest_python_support_updated_plus_retained
answer: pytest dropped support for Python 3.7, so Python 3.8+ remained supported. Curated benchmark note from the official changelog.

name: pytest_python_ceiling_updated_plus_retained
answer: At that point, Python 3.13 was the newest explicitly supported Python line in the changelog. pytest 8.4.0 added support for Python 3.13, described as support for Python 3.13 beta1 at the time of writing.

name: fastapi_pydantic_migration_updated_plus_retained
answer: The migration target still remains Pydantic v2. FastAPI 0.119.0 supports both Pydantic v2 models and pydantic.v1 models at the same time in the same app to ease migration.

name: typer_python_support_updated_plus_retained
answer: Before the later breaking cleanup, Typer still supported Python 3.9+, while the release stream was already upgrading types for Python 3.10 and adding internal scripts to migrate docs from Python 3.9 to Python 3.10.

name: httpx_app_shortcut_updated_plus_retained
answer: In HTTPX 0.27.0, the `app=...` shortcut was still present but deprecated. The release notes told users to prefer the explicit transport style with `transport=httpx.WSGITransport()` or `transport=httpx.ASGITransport()` instead.

name: httpx_proxy_cleanup_updated_plus_retained
answer: The old proxies argument would still continue to work for the moment, even though HTTPX 0.26.0 added the replacement proxy argument and deprecated proxies. The documented path was already to use proxy or mounts for more complex configurations.

name: uvicorn_python_support_updated_plus_retained
answer: Before the later cleanup, Uvicorn still supported Python 3.9+, and the release stream had already added support for Python 3.14. Version window: 0.39.0 before the Python 3.9 drop.

name: pydantic_ai_python_support_updated_plus_retained
answer: Before v1.0.0, Pydantic AI still supported Python 3.9+, and the same guide already documented support for ThinkingPart entries in v0.3.0.

name: fastapi_python_ceiling_updated_plus_retained
answer: FastAPI 0.119.0 added support for Python 3.14, while Python 3.13 still remained supported in the same later release-note stream. At that point, Python 3.13 was the newest explicitly supported Python line in the release notes.

name: pytest_python_ceiling_updated_plus_retained
answer: pytest 9.0.0 added official support for Python 3.14, while Python 3.13 still remained supported in the same later changelog stream. At that point, Python 3.13 was the newest explicitly supported Python line in the changelog.

name: httpx_app_shortcut_updated_plus_retained
answer: The explicit transport style with `transport=httpx.WSGITransport()` or `transport=httpx.ASGITransport()` remained the documented path after the removal. In HTTPX 0.27.0, the `app=...` shortcut was still present but deprecated.

name: httpx_proxy_cleanup_updated_plus_retained
answer: The old proxies argument would still continue to work for the moment, even though HTTPX 0.26.0 added the replacement proxy argument and deprecated proxies. The documented path was already to use proxy or mounts for more complex configurations.

name: uvicorn_python_support_updated_plus_retained
answer: Uvicorn later dropped Python 3.9, so Python 3.10+ remained supported. Before the later cleanup, Uvicorn still supported Python 3.9+, and the release stream had already added support for Python 3.14.

name: pydantic_ai_python_support_updated_plus_retained
answer: Before v1.0.0, Pydantic AI still supported Python 3.9+, and the same guide already documented support for ThinkingPart entries in v0.3.0. Pydantic AI v1.0.0 dropped support for Python 3.9, so Python 3.10+ remained supported.

name: fastapi_pydantic_migration_conflict_synthesis_1
answer: Since FastAPI 0.119.0, there is also partial support for Pydantic v1 from inside of Pydantic v2, to facilitate the migration to v2. Pydantic v2 still remains the supported migration target, and FastAPI later ran performance tests only on Pydantic v2.
